design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Selective-inference pivot for IDA/MPDAG possible causal effects. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are IDA and joint-IDA possible-effect multisets (Maathuis-Kalisch-Buhlmann), Perkovic/Guo-Perkovic minimal enumeration for MPDAG possible effects, and Lee-Sun-Sun-Taylor polyhedral selective inference after model selection. Why non-trivial? The target is not another MPDAG orientation criterion or adjustment-set enumeration: require a hand-derived polyhedral selection certificate for the event that a particular DAG completion/effect is the reported extremal possible effect, and an exact truncated-Gaussian pivot for inference on that selected extremal causal effect under linear-Gaussian SEMs. Why promising? The nonroutine object is estimator/inference geometry: the IDA max-effect selection event becomes a finite intersection of affine inequalities in regression coefficient estimates, with a two-completion witness showing naive Wald coverage fails while the selective pivot is exact. If the delta reduces to IDA enumeration, standard adjustment, constrained-gradient geometry, or ordinary post-selection inference without a causal-effect selection map, pivot or accept field-tier.

proposed_from.topic: Selective-inference pivot for IDA/MPDAG possible causal effects. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are IDA and joint-IDA possible-effect multisets (Maathuis-Kalisch-Buhlmann), Perkovic/Guo-Perkovic minimal enumeration for MPDAG possible effects, and Lee-Sun-Sun-Taylor polyhedral selective inference after model selection. Why non-trivial? The target is not another MPDAG orientation criterion or adjustment-set enumeration: require a hand-derived polyhedral selection certificate for the event that a particular DAG completion/effect is the reported extremal possible effect, and an exact truncated-Gaussian pivot for inference on that selected extremal causal effect under linear-Gaussian SEMs. Why promising? The nonroutine object is estimator/inference geometry: the IDA max-effect selection event becomes a finite intersection of affine inequalities in regression coefficient estimates, with a two-completion witness showing naive Wald coverage fails while the selective pivot is exact. If the delta reduces to IDA enumeration, standard adjustment, constrained-gradient geometry, or ordinary post-selection inference without a causal-effect selection map, pivot or accept field-tier.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The Stage -1.1 prior-proposal map found no direct CausalSmith proposal for IDA, MPDAG, CPDAG, or possible-effect selective pivots. The closest in-repo analogues are eid_proximal_phase_v1 on regularizer-selected ATE artifacts, eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier_v1 on localized report-class inference frontiers, and pid_dose_response_iv_v1 on an adjacent-rank sign certificate; none states a selective confidence region for a deduplicated IDA/MPDAG possible-effect set or the minimum-absolute lower-bound score.","published_axis":"MaathuisKalischBuhlmann2009IDA and GuoPerkovic2021MinimalEnumeration define and deduplicate possible-effect reports, but do not condition on the event that a particular deduplicated set and lower-bound score were selected. LeeSunSunTaylor2016Polyhedral gives exact inference only after the affine event is known. The epsilon-gap is the missing IDA-specific selection certificate A_s,b_s for graph learning, duplicate partitioning, and nonsmooth lower-bound reporting, plus the simultaneous selected-set and selected-score pivot."}
seed_list: Derive a polyhedral certificate for the event that a named minimally enumerated IDA/MPDAG branch is the reported extremal possible effect, then conjecture a truncated-Gaussian selective pivot for that selected effect.
Construct selective confidence regions for the deduplicated IDA/MPDAG possible-effect set and the min-absolute lower-bound score after graph learning and minimal enumeration.
Give a multivariate selective pivot for joint-IDA vector branches under compatible parent-set collections in CPDAGs/MPDAGs.
Derive a two-completion witness proving naive Wald coverage fails for the selected IDA extremal branch under local competing branch means.
Characterize the exact tie and near-tie truncation geometry for IDA min-absolute possible-effect reports.
Build a polyhedral randomized graph-screen variant of IDA whose selected possible-effect report has finite-sample exact selective inference.
Bridge Guo-Perkovic minimal enumeration and Lee selective inference by giving an algorithm that outputs branch contrast eta and selection matrix A for every extremal possible-effect report.

Extend the selected-functional mismatch of post-causal-discovery inference to IDA by proving the selected estimand is theta_mstar rather than the true total effect outside singleton equivalence classes.
Develop simultaneous selective intervals for all minimally enumerated possible effects with familywise coverage conditional on the selected CPDAG/MPDAG.
Give a selective pivot for the IDA lower-bound score min_m |theta_m| by conditioning on the signed branch attaining the nonsmooth minimum.

seed_details.one_line: Derive a polyhedral certificate for the event that a named minimally enumerated IDA/MPDAG branch is the reported extremal possible effect, then conjecture a truncated-Gaussian selective pivot for that selected effect.

anchor_paper.author: Jason D. Lee, Dennis L. Sun, Yuekai Sun, Jonathan E. Taylor
anchor_paper.claim: Exact post-selection inference is available for Gaussian linear contrasts when the selection event is an affine polyhedron.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: conditional on the event {Ay <= b}, eta^T y has a truncated normal distribution

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends post-selection inference to IDA, but the nontrivial object is the hand-derived causal-effect selection map A_{m,s}.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from LeeSunSunTaylor2016Polyhedral because IDA branch selection just chooses the largest regression coefficient.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Lee et al. require Ay<=b and a fixed contrast, while IDA first creates branch functionals from MPDAG completion/minimal-enumeration logic and selects a causal functional.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not LeeSunSunTaylor2016Polyhedral because it constructs the missing IDA extremal-branch certificate and selected causal contrast rather than assuming them.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the output is a new certificate A_{m,s} plus a selected-functional pivot, not ordinary post-selection inference on a pre-named coefficient.

adversarial_referee.instance: May lack computable evaluation map for IDA selection.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebutted by explicit A_{m,s} rows t a_k - s a_m and Exhibit 9.1.

adversarial_referee.instance: Graph learning event may be nonpolyhedral.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Lands partly; finite branch certificate is theorem, full graph-selected pivot is conjecture under polyhedral-screen assumption.

adversarial_referee.instance: Lee targets lasso-style selected contrasts, not causal effects.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: That difference is the gap: the proposal supplies the IDA causal-effect selection map needed before Lee applies.

seed_details.burned_reason: Angle 0 could not rise above field tier after repeated revisions; angle 1 was not-publishable. Reviewers found promissory affine discovery certificates, missing concrete PC/GES witness rows, and insufficient novelty beyond Lee selective inference plus IDA enumeration.

seed_details.one_line: Construct selective confidence regions for the deduplicated IDA/MPDAG possible-effect set and the min-absolute lower-bound score after graph learning and minimal enumeration.

anchor_paper.author: Richard Guo, Emilija Perkovic
anchor_paper.claim: Minimal enumeration targets distinct possible-effect functionals because duplicate reports can arise under sampling noise.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: enumerate all possible total effects with duplicates removed

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation is stronger because min |theta| is nonsmooth and tie-sensitive, not a routine ATE restatement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just Bonferroni over GuoPerkovic2021MinimalEnumeration branches with ordinary regression intervals.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the min-absolute report is selected and nonsmooth, and duplicate merging/ties alter the conditioning event.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not GuoPerkovic2021MinimalEnumeration because it targets selective coverage for the deduplicated set and its nonsmooth lower-bound score.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if the confidence region includes the tie-aware selection certificate; otherwise lands as field-tier.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could cover max, min, set, and score jointly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: seed explicitly names both deduplicated set and min-absolute score.

adversarial_referee.instance: Tie witness could sit exactly at zero.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use local near-tie open neighborhoods, not exact zero-only examples.

adversarial_referee.instance: No computable truncation map yet for set-valued report.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Weak; downgrade behind seed 1 until a concrete map is derived.

seed_details.burned_reason: Angle 0 could not rise above field tier after repeated revisions; angle 1 was not-publishable. Reviewers found promissory affine discovery certificates, missing concrete PC/GES witness rows, and insufficient novelty beyond Lee selective inference plus IDA enumeration.

seed_details.one_line: Give a multivariate selective pivot for joint-IDA vector branches under compatible parent-set collections in CPDAGs/MPDAGs.

anchor_paper.author: Preetam Nandy, Marloes H. Maathuis, Thomas S. Richardson
anchor_paper.claim: Joint-IDA estimates effects of multiple simultaneous interventions under unknown linear SEMs.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: estimate the total effect of multiple simultaneous interventions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: CATE/vector variant warning, but compatibility constraints make coordinatewise pivots invalid.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is coordinatewise Lee inference applied to each joint-IDA coefficient.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: joint-IDA branches require jointly valid parent-set collections, so coordinatewise conditioning ignores compatibility constraints.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not NandyMaathuisRichardson2017JointIDA because it conditions on the selected compatible parent-set collection and vector report.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS only with a simultaneous truncation or Gaussian cone object; otherwise routine coordinatewise inference.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Lands; keep as second-angle candidate, not chosen.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could include point and joint interventions jointly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The seed explicitly targets joint-IDA vector family.

adversarial_referee.instance: Guo-Perkovic 2022 may already cover efficient LS.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: They cover estimation under MPDAGs, not selection-conditioned vector inference.

seed_details.one_line: Derive a two-completion witness proving naive Wald coverage fails for the selected IDA extremal branch under local competing branch means.

anchor_paper.author: Marloes H. Maathuis, Markus Kalisch, Peter Buhlmann
anchor_paper.claim: IDA estimates a multiset of possible causal effects from an estimated equivalence class.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: a collection of estimated causal effects

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample is useful only if nondegenerate and tied to named IDA reporting; the local-mean witness does that.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just winner's curse for two Gaussian means.
seed_details.rebuttal: Partly true; as a standalone seed it is field-tier, but as a supporting theorem it diagnoses IDA extremal-effect reporting.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the exact tie and near-tie truncation geometry for IDA min-absolute possible-effect reports.

anchor_paper.author: Marloes H. Maathuis, Markus Kalisch, Peter Buhlmann
anchor_paper.claim: IDA uses the minimum absolute possible effect as a lower-bound score.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: minimum absolute value of the estimated possible causal effects

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift is nonroutine only if it gives a usable tie-aware truncation map.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just absolute-value selection over signed Gaussian means.
seed_details.rebuttal: The causal branch list and duplicate-minimal enumeration make the signed list data-dependent, but standalone novelty is modest.

seed_details.one_line: Build a polyhedral randomized graph-screen variant of IDA whose selected possible-effect report has finite-sample exact selective inference.

anchor_paper.author: Paula Gradu, Tijana Zrnic, Yixin Wang, Michael I. Jordan
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Valid inference after causal discovery accounts for double dipping through graph-selection procedures.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment-process variation becomes nontrivial if randomization creates a finite-sample polyhedron rather than a generic correction.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just randomized selective inference after graph selection.
seed_details.rebuttal: The new piece is mapping randomized graph output to an IDA possible-effect branch and selected target.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not GraduZrnicWangJordan2025ValidInference because the selected object is an extremal possible-effect functional rather than graph membership alone.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: Weak: without a concrete randomized graph-screen algorithm it risks being ordinary randomized selection inference; downgraded.

seed_details.one_line: Bridge Guo-Perkovic minimal enumeration and Lee selective inference by giving an algorithm that outputs branch contrast eta and selection matrix A for every extremal possible-effect report.

anchor_paper.author: Richard Guo, Emilija Perkovic
anchor_paper.claim: Possible total effects in an MPDAG can be enumerated minimally as distinct functionals.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: minimal enumeration of all possible total effects

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Algorithmic-identifiability is stronger if complexity and output certificate are explicit.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a software wrapper around minimal enumeration plus linear inequalities.
seed_details.rebuttal: The algorithmic certificate is valuable but may be engineering-level unless paired with exact pivot theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Extend the selected-functional mismatch of post-causal-discovery inference to IDA by proving the selected estimand is theta_mstar rather than the true total effect outside singleton equivalence classes.

anchor_paper.author: Ting-Hsuan Chang, Zijian Guo, Daniel Malinsky
anchor_paper.claim: Selected graphs may define selected functionals different from the true causal effect.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: selected graphs may define selected functionals different from the true causal effect

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: New-object seed is more conceptual than flagship unless tied to a pivot or coverage theorem.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is exactly the selected-functional point in ChangGuoMalinsky2026PostSelectionCausalDiscovery.
seed_details.rebuttal: As a standalone theorem it is too close; use only as positioning for the selected IDA target.

seed_details.one_line: Develop simultaneous selective intervals for all minimally enumerated possible effects with familywise coverage conditional on the selected CPDAG/MPDAG.

anchor_paper.author: Richard Guo, Emilija Perkovic
anchor_paper.claim: Minimal enumeration removes duplicate possible-effect functionals before reporting the set.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Joint-family seed is stronger than one-branch inference but risks routine multiplicity correction.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just selective Bonferroni or Scheffe over the branch list.
seed_details.rebuttal: Could fail; needs dependence-aware truncation geometry to be more than multiplicity correction.

seed_details.one_line: Give a selective pivot for the IDA lower-bound score min_m |theta_m| by conditioning on the signed branch attaining the nonsmooth minimum.

anchor_paper.author: Marloes H. Maathuis, Markus Kalisch, Peter Buhlmann
anchor_paper.claim: IDA reports minimum absolute possible effect as a lower-bound causal-importance score.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation is weaker than max-effect branch because minimum absolute value has nuisance near-zero instability.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is one-dimensional absolute-value selective inference after choosing the nearest coefficient to zero.
seed_details.rebuttal: The branch-generation layer is causal, but the inferential geometry may be too routine; keep as field-tier fallback.

proposed_from.literature_map: Maathuis-Kalisch-Buhlmann 2009 Annals introduced IDA, estimating a collection of possible total effects from a CPDAG and using minimum absolute possible effect as a causal-importance lower bound. Nandy-Maathuis-Richardson 2017 Annals extended IDA to joint interventions with vector possible effects and asymptotic theory. Perkovic-Kalisch-Maathuis 2017 UAI adapted IDA and joint-IDA to MPDAGs with background knowledge. Guo-Perkovic 2021 AISTATS gave minimal enumeration of distinct possible total-effect functionals, removing duplicate branch reports. Guo-Perkovic 2022 JMLR gave efficient recursive least squares for identified total effects under MPDAGs. Lee-Sun-Sun-Taylor 2016 Annals gave exact Gaussian selective inference for a linear contrast conditional on a polyhedral event Ay <= b. Tibshirani-Taylor-Lockhart-Tibshirani 2016 JASA showed sequential regression can also have polyhedral selection geometry. Gradu-Zrnic-Wang-Jordan 2025 JASA and Chang-Guo-Malinsky 2026 Biometrika show post-causal-discovery inference must correct double dipping. Chang-Guo-Malinsky also emphasize selected-functional mismatch after graph selection. The open junction is exact or sharp selective inference for the IDA/MPDAG possible-effect object itself, combining graph discovery, branch enumeration, extremal reporting, and regression estimation.
proposed_from.last_draft_handoff: {"status":"completed","mode":"pivot","version":1,"message":"Selective confidence regions for the deduplicated IDA/MPDAG possible-effect set and its minimum-absolute lower-bound score.","literature_map":"Maathuis-Kalisch-Buhlmann 2009 introduced IDA: estimate a multiset of possible total causal effects from an estimated CPDAG under linear/Gaussian, causally sufficient assumptions. IDA reports set summaries, especially the minimum absolute possible effect, as a lower-bound causal-importance score when the effect is not uniquely identified. Nandy-Maathuis-Richardson 2017 extends this to joint interventions, producing vectors of possible joint effects and asymptotic theory under sparse linear SEMs. Perkovic-Kalisch-Maathuis 2017 moves from CPDAGs to MPDAGs with background knowledge and adapts IDA/joint-IDA style methods. Guo-Perkovic 2021 resolves minimal enumeration of distinct possible total-effect functionals and removes duplicate possible-effect reports caused by sampling variability. Guo-Perkovic 2022 gives efficient recursive least squares for identified total effects under MPDAGs, including point and joint interventions. Lee-Sun-Sun-Taylor 2016 supplies the core polyhedral selective-inference pivot: a Gaussian linear contrast conditional on Ay <= b has a truncated-Gaussian law. Tibshirani-Taylor-Lockhart-Tibshirani 2016 shows the same geometry-driven requirement for tractable sequential-regression selection events. Gradu-Zrnic-Wang-Jordan 2025 and Chang-Guo-Malinsky 2026 show post-causal-discovery inference needs correction for double dipping after graph selection. Chang-Guo-Malinsky also stresses selected-functional mismatch: selected graphs may define selected functionals different from the true causal effect. The unresolved junction is selective inference for the IDA/MPDAG possible-effect object itself: graph discovery, minimal enumeration, deduplication, lower-bound reporting, and regression estimation in banked_reason: Stopped early after the selective-IDA topic failed to reach flagship: angle 0 stayed field-tier through three revisions and was killed as nonflagship, then angle 1 rejected as not-publishable; the proposal did not deliver a concrete discovery-rule certificate or a flagship post-selection causal inference object.

seeds_burned.one_liner: Derive a polyhedral certificate for the event that a named minimally enumerated IDA/MPDAG branch is the reported extremal possible effect, then conjecture a truncated-Gaussian selective pivot for that selected effect.
seeds_burned.reason: Angle 0 could not rise above field tier after repeated revisions; angle 1 was not-publishable. Reviewers found promissory affine discovery certificates, missing concrete PC/GES witness rows, and insufficient novelty beyond Lee selective inference plus IDA enumeration.

seeds_burned.one_liner: Construct selective confidence regions for the deduplicated IDA/MPDAG possible-effect set and the min-absolute lower-bound score after graph learning and minimal enumeration.
seeds_burned.reason: Angle 0 could not rise above field tier after repeated revisions; angle 1 was not-publishable. Reviewers found promissory affine discovery certificates, missing concrete PC/GES witness rows, and insufficient novelty beyond Lee selective inference plus IDA enumeration.
